title: NOC 32129 – Other Medical Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Other Medical Technologists and Technicians under NOC 32129 include specialized allied health professionals working in dietetics, prosthetics, orthotics, ocular prosthetics, and related medical technical fields across Canada. As healthcare services expand and rehabilitation demand increases, this TEER 2 occupation continues to offer stable employment prospects. For internationally trained professionals, NOC 32129 provides eligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs, offering a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3212 – Medical technologists and techniciansNOC Code32129

duties: Assist dietitians in planning and supervising food service operations
Paint artificial eyes to match natural appearance
Clean, repair, and maintain ocular prosthetics
Design and fit artificial limbs, braces, and supports
Build and assemble prosthetic and orthotic devices
Use materials such as metals, plastics, and composites
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- to three-year college program in dietary technology
Completion of training from the American Society of OcularistsOR
Certification by the National Examining Board of Ocularists is usually required
Two-year college program in prosthetics and orthotics technology
Three years of supervised practical training
Certification by the Canadian Board of Certification of Prosthetists and Orthotists may be required
Secondary school plus supervised practical training
Registration with the Canadian Association of Prosthetists and Orthotists may be required
Internationally educated professionals must complete credential evaluation and certification processes prior to employment.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $28,600 to $57,300, depending on specialization and province.
job_outlook: Aging population and increased rehabilitation needs
Growth in prosthetic and orthotic device demand
Professionals with certification and hands-on experience may experience stronger employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Nursing homes and community care facilities
This positive gap suggests favorable employment prospects, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and Manitoba.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32129?
Other Medical Technologists and Technicians benefit from:
Growing rehabilitation and assistive device demand
Stable public and private sector roles
As Canada’s population ages and rehabilitation services expand, demand for prosthetics, orthotics, and clinical support services continues to grow.
pathways: If you are qualified in dietetics technology, prosthetics, orthotics, or ocular technology, NOC 32129 may provide a pathway toward permanent residence in Canada.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 32129.
